description_long: This is a freestanding stone wall constructed from irregularly shaped, roughly hewn stones of varying sizes and colors, predominantly in shades of beige, tan, and light gray. The stones are tightly stacked in a dry-laid pattern, with visible gaps and uneven surfaces, giving the wall a rustic and traditional appearance. The base of the wall features a slightly protruding stone ledge, providing additional stability and a subtle visual accent. Both the front and back faces of the wall display the same stonework, with no visible openings or decorative embellishments. The wall is relatively thin in profile, as seen from the side views, and appears to be designed as a boundary or partition in an outdoor urban or park setting. The surface texture is rough and tactile, with the natural contours of the stones creating a visually interesting and durable barrier.
description: A rustic, freestanding stone wall made of irregular beige and gray stones, tightly stacked in a traditional dry-laid pattern with a rough, textured surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the uneven, natural stone faces and the protruding stone base ledge.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the wall's thin profile and the layered arrangement of stones.
description_view_2: Back view similar to the front, displaying the same stonework and rough texture.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the wall's narrow thickness and stone layering.
